# ContextBoundary

> Open-source egress governance specification for AI data flows. Deployment-agnostic, regulatory-mapped, enforceable at the network layer.

ContextBoundary is one of three sibling open-source projects under github.com/kannanokannan that together define an AI governance stack:

- ContextOps — How does an organisation govern its AI context?
- ContextBoundary — Where is data allowed to go?
- Sthala — Where does the AI actually run?

License: Apache 2.0. Status: v0.1 active.

## Core

- [FRAMEWORK.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/FRAMEWORK.md): Core specification
- [RATIONALE.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/RATIONALE.md): Why this exists — sovereignty gap, design decisions
- [tier-classification.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/tier-classification.md): Egress Tier I/II/III definitions
- [boundary-diagram.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/boundary-diagram.md): Five-zone canonical diagram
- [vendor-tier-matrix.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/vendor-tier-matrix.md): Pipeline stages mapped to vendor zones
- [gdpr.md](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary/blob/main/gdpr.md): GDPR Audit Profile

## Related projects

- [ContextOps](https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextOps): AI context governance framework
- [Sthala](https://github.com/kannanokannan/Sthala): Sovereign on-prem AI reference framework
